expectations.text: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report identifies or classifies GOLD/Barrick as a commodity producer or miner
expectations.evidence: Title: 'B (Barrick Mining)'. Executive Summary: 'Barrick is still fundamentally levered to gold'

expectations.text: Report discusses gold commodity price before or prominently alongside company analysis
expectations.evidence: Rationale line 17: 'gold up +1.61% intraday to ~$5,186/oz' and Exec Summary: 'gold remains the main driver here (80-90% of the story)'. Not a dedicated section but prominently alongside.

expectations.text: Report mentions AISC (all-in sustaining cost) or production cost/breakeven
expectations.evidence: Rationale line 17: 'Barrick FY2025 gold AISC was $1,637/oz'

expectations.text: Report explains miner leverage to underlying commodity (how gold moves amplify in miners)
expectations.evidence: Bearish Catalysts line 78: 'miners are operating leverage vehicles, so a commodity pullback can hit equity multiples and earnings fast' — this MENTIONS leverage but does not EXPLAIN the mechanism or quantify it. No calculation showing how gold % changes amplify into miner equity moves.

expectations.text: Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit BUY, SELL, or HOLD action
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report has a Valuation Summary section
expectations.evidence: Lines 46-56: Valuation Summary with P/E, EV/EBITDA, P/S (though Historical Avg columns are all N/A)

expectations.text: Report discusses gold price trend or outlook as part of the thesis
expectations.evidence: Catalyst line 74: 'Sustained gold strength above $5,000/oz through 2026 keeps miner cash generation elevated'

expectations.text: Report has a Sources section with references
Recommendation rationale connects back to the gold commodity thesis
expectations.evidence: Rationale line 17: 'Commodity-producer setup is strong (gold up +1.61% intraday to ~$5,186/oz while Barrick FY2025 gold AISC was $1,637/oz)'

claims.evidence: Matches with_skill output and Barrick IR

claims.claim: Valuation Historical Avg: N/A for all metrics
claims.evidence: Old skill failed to retrieve historical valuation averages — with_skill found them (14.87x, 10.00x, 6.02x)

suggestions.assertion: Report discusses gold commodity price before or prominently alongside company analysis
suggestions.reason: This passed despite there being no dedicated commodity section — gold is mentioned in the Rationale but not analyzed separately. The with_skill version has a full '## Gold Commodity Price Analysis (First)' section. A stricter assertion ('Report has a dedicated section analyzing the gold commodity before stock analysis') would discriminate better.

No assertion checks for historical valuation averages. The old skill returned N/A for all three historical average columns while the with_skill provided actual numbers — a meaningful quality gap that goes undetected.

eval_feedback.overall: 10/11 passed. The single failure (miner leverage explanation) correctly identifies a genuine gap — the old skill mentions leverage in passing but doesn't explain the mechanism. The with_skill version includes a quantitative calculation. Several other qualitative differences (dedicated commodity section, historical valuation data) pass the assertions but represent real quality gaps.
